summary: Post-external-review fixes: (1) fixed set -e exit code capture bug in verify_python_base.sh using the || exit_code=$? idiom, matching the pattern already applied in verify_venv_builder.sh and verify_keystone.sh; (2) corrected docker build tag names in container-images.md docs to use bare names (python-base, venv-builder) matching FROM directives in downstream Dockerfiles, added dual-tag code block for canonical registry tags; (3) added two review pattern files and the external review record.

tests_checklist.item: Exit code capture pattern is consistent across all verify scripts

code_quality_checklist.item: No dead code or commented-out code

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible

suggested_improvements: tests/scripts/test_apply_constraint_overrides.sh:39-40 has the same set -e / $? bug pattern: `(cd "$workdir" && bash "$SCRIPT_UNDER_TEST" "2025.2")` followed by `local exit_code=$?`. If the subshell fails, set -e aborts before exit_code is captured. This is outside the current diff scope but directly contradicts the newly added review pattern 'apply-bug-fixes-to-all-instances-of-the-same-pattern.md'. Consider applying the || exit_code=$? fix there too.
next_steps: Apply the || exit_code=$? fix to tests/scripts/test_apply_constraint_overrides.sh for full consistency with the new review pattern

metadata.title: C001: Add Keystone container image and release config
